title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: Welcome nysOpenData package, R package dedicated helping R users connect NY State Data Portal! nysOpenData package provides streamlined interface accessing New York State’s vast open data resources. connects directly NY State Open Data Portal, helping users bridge gap raw APIs tidy data analysis. two ways:

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: primary way pull data package nys_pull_dataset() function, works tandem nys_list_datasets(). need know anything API keys authentication. first step call nys_list_datasets() see datasets list available use nys_pull_dataset() function. provides information thousands datasets found portal. output includes columns dataset title, description, link source. important pieces key uid. need either order use nys_pull_dataset() function. can put either key value uid value dataset = filter inside nys_pull_dataset(). instance, want pull dataset Lottery Cash 4 Life Winning Numbers: Beginning 2014, can use either methods : matter put uid key value dataset =, successfully get data!
code: nys_list_datasets() |> head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 18 #>   key        uid   title accessLevel landingPage issued `@type` modified keyword #>   <chr>      <chr> <chr> <chr>       <chr>       <chr>  <chr>   <chr>    <list>  #> 1 state_of_… 22ew… Stat… public      https://da… 2016-… dcat:D… 2019-06… <chr>   #> 2 open_ny_q… 22ie… Open… public      https://da… 2022-… dcat:D… 2025-06… <chr>   #> 3 lobbying_… 238s… Lobb… public      https://da… 2018-… dcat:D… 2023-07… <chr>   #> 4 mta_subwa… 23fs… MTA … public      https://da… 2025-… dcat:D… 2026-03… <chr>   #> 5 alternati… 23ry… Alte… public      https://da… 2023-… dcat:D… 2026-03… <NULL>  #> 6 nys_thruw… 23v4… NYS … public      https://da… 2017-… dcat:D… 2020-01… <chr>   #> # ℹ 9 more variables: identifier <chr>, description <chr>, distribution <list>, #> #   theme <list>, `contactPoint.@type` <chr>, contactPoint.fn <chr>, #> #   contactPoint.hasEmail <chr>, `publisher.@type` <chr>, publisher.name <chr> nys_cash_for_life_uid <- nys_pull_dataset(   dataset = "kwxv-fwze", limit = 2)  nys_cash_for_life_key <- nys_pull_dataset(   dataset = "lottery_cash_4_life_winning_numbers_beginning_2014", limit = 2)

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: easiest workflow use nys_list_datasets() together nys_pull_dataset(). However, ample datasets portal, new ones added time, list datasets. event particular dataset want use R list, can use nys_any_dataset(). requirement dataset’s API endpoint (URL provided nys Open Data portal). steps get : NY State Open Data Portal, go dataset want work . Click “Export” (next actions button right hand side). Click “API Endpoint”. Click “SODA2” “Version”. Copy API Endpoint. example use nys_any_dataset() API endpoint discovered, pull data nys_pull_dataset() example:

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: functions provide access NY State Open Data, serve slightly different purposes. general: Use nys_pull_dataset() dataset available nys_list_datasets() Use nys_any_dataset() working datasets outside catalog Together, functions allow users either quickly access datasets flexibly query dataset available nys Open Data portal.

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: Every day, New York State hosts Cash 4 Life lottery, dates winning numbers can found . R, nysOpenData package can used pull data directly. Let’s filter dataset include rows cash_ball “04”. nys_pull_dataset() function can filter based columns dataset. filter, add filters = list() put whatever filters like inside. colnames call , know column called “cash_ball” can use accomplish . Success! calling lottery_04 dataset see 2 rows data, distinct() call see cash_ball featured dataset “04.” can also add one criteria filtering. successfully filtered latest two rows winning number either “01” “02”
code: lottery_04 <- nys_pull_dataset(dataset = "kwxv-fwze",limit = 2, filters = list(cash_ball = "04")) lottery_04 #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   draw_date           winning_numbers cash_ball #>   <dttm>              <chr>               <dbl> #> 1 2026-02-21 00:00:00 20 25 30 52 55          4 #> 2 2026-02-17 00:00:00 11 22 32 56 60          4  # Checking to see the filtering worked lottery_04 |>   distinct(cash_ball) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   cash_ball #>       <dbl> #> 1         4 lottery_01_02 <- nys_pull_dataset(dataset = "kwxv-fwze",limit = 2, filters = list(cash_ball = c("01","02"))) lottery_01_02 #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   draw_date           winning_numbers cash_ball #>   <dttm>              <chr>               <dbl> #> 1 2026-02-20 00:00:00 02 28 49 50 56          1 #> 2 2026-02-18 00:00:00 03 28 38 40 49          1

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: Now successfully pulled data R, let’s mini analysis using cash_ball column, figure top cash_ball winners. , create bar graph Cash Ball frequencies. Frequency Cash Ball numbers among recent New York Cash 4 Life lottery winning tickets. Bars ordered least frequent highlight distribution outcomes. graph shows us cash balls won, many won.
code: # Visualizing the distribution, ordered by frequency  lottery <- nys_pull_dataset(dataset = "kwxv-fwze",limit = 50)  lottery |>   count(cash_ball) |>   ggplot(aes(     x = n,     y = reorder(cash_ball, n)   )) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   theme_minimal() +   labs(     title = "Top 50 Cash Ball Numbers in Winning Lottery Tickets",     x = "Number of Winners",     y = "Cash Ball"   )

title: Getting Started with nysOpenData
text: nysOpenData package serves robust interface NY State Open Data portal, streamlining path raw city APIs actionable insights. abstracting complexities data acquisition—pagination, type-casting, complex filtering—allows users focus analysis rather data engineering. demonstrated vignette, package provides seamless workflow targeted data retrieval, automated filtering, rapid visualization.

title: nysOpenData
text: nysOpenData provides simple, reproducible access datasets NY State Open Data platform — directly R, API keys manual downloads required. package available CRAN. Version 0.1.1 introduces streamlined, catalog-driven interface NYS Open Data. package provides three core functions: nys_list_datasets() — Browse available datasets live nys Open Data catalog nys_pull_dataset() — Pull cataloged dataset key, filtering, ordering, optional date controls nys_any_dataset() — Pull NYS Open Data dataset directly via Socrata JSON endpoint Datasets pulled via nys_pull_dataset() automatically apply sensible defaults catalog (default ordering date fields), still allowing user control : limit filters date / / order clean_names coerce_types redesign reduces maintenance burden, improves extensibility, provides scalable interface working nys Open Data. functions return clean tibble outputs support filtering viafilters = list(field = "value").

title: nysOpenData
text: nysOpenData makes New York State’s civic datasets accessible students, educators, analysts, researchers unified user-friendly R interface. Developed support reproducible research, open-data literacy, real-world analysis.

title: nysOpenData
text: RSocrata package provides general interface Socrata-backed portal, nysOpenData specifically tailored New York State Open Data. package part broader ecosystem tools working New York open data: nysOpenData — streamlined access NYC Open Data nysOpenData — streamlined access NY State Open Data Together, packages provide consistent, user-friendly interface working civic data across jurisdictions. Ease Use: need hunt 4x4 dataset IDs (e.g., kwxv-fwze); use catalog-based keys instead. Open Literacy: Designed specifically students researchers lower barrier entry civic data analysis.

title: nysOpenData
text: welcome contributions! find bug like request wrapper specific nys dataset, please open issue submit pull request GitHub.

what: Load Any NYS Open Data Dataset — nys_any_dataset
title: Load Any NYS Open Data Dataset — nys_any_dataset
text: Downloads NYS Open Data dataset given Socrata JSON endpoint.

title: Load Any NYS Open Data Dataset — nys_any_dataset
code: nys_any_dataset(   json_link,   limit = 10000,   timeout_sec = 30,   clean_names = TRUE,   coerce_types = TRUE )

title: Load Any NYS Open Data Dataset — nys_any_dataset
text: json_link Socrata dataset JSON endpoint URL (e.g., "https://data.ny.gov/resource/28gk-bu58.json"). limit Number rows retrieve (default = 10,000). timeout_sec Request timeout seconds (default = 30). clean_names Logical; TRUE, convert column names snake_case (default = TRUE). coerce_types Logical; TRUE, attempt light type coercion (default = TRUE).

title: Load Any NYS Open Data Dataset — nys_any_dataset
text: tibble containing requested dataset.

title: Load Any NYS Open Data Dataset — nys_any_dataset
code: # Examples that hit the live nys Open Data API are guarded so CRAN checks # do not fail when the network is unavailable or slow. if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   endpoint <- "https://data.ny.gov/resource/28gk-bu58.json"   out <- try(nys_any_dataset(endpoint, limit = 3), silent = TRUE)   if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) {     head(out)   } }

what: List datasets available in nysOpenData — nys_list_datasets
title: List datasets available in nysOpenData — nys_list_datasets
text: Retrieves current Open NY catalog returns datasets available use `nys_pull_dataset()`.

title: List datasets available in nysOpenData — nys_list_datasets
List datasets available in nysOpenData — nys_list_datasets
text: tibble available datasets, including generated `key`, dataset   `uid`, dataset `title`.

title: List datasets available in nysOpenData — nys_list_datasets
text: Keys generated dataset titles using `janitor::make_clean_names()`.

title: List datasets available in nysOpenData — nys_list_datasets

what: Pull a NYS Open Data dataset from the NYS Open Data catalog — nys_pull_dataset
title: Pull a NYS Open Data dataset from the NYS Open Data catalog — nys_pull_dataset
text: Uses dataset `key` `uid` `nys_list_datasets()` pull data NYS Open Data.

title: Pull a NYS Open Data dataset from the NYS Open Data catalog — nys_pull_dataset
code: nys_pull_dataset(   dataset,   limit = 10000,   filters = list(),   date = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   date_field = NULL,   where = NULL,   order = NULL,   timeout_sec = 30,   clean_names = TRUE,   coerce_types = TRUE )

title: Pull a NYS Open Data dataset from the NYS Open Data catalog — nys_pull_dataset
text: dataset dataset key UID `nys_list_datasets()`. limit Number rows retrieve (default = 10,000). filters Optional named list filters. Supports vectors (translated ()). date Optional single date (matches times day) using `date_field`. Optional start date (inclusive) using `date_field`. Optional end date (exclusive) using `date_field`. date_field Optional date/datetime column use `date`, ``, ``. Must supplied `date`, ``, `` used. Optional raw SoQL clause. `date`, ``, `` provided, conditions -ed . order Optional SoQL ORDER clause. timeout_sec Request timeout seconds (default = 30). clean_names Logical; TRUE, convert column names snake_case (default = TRUE). coerce_types Logical; TRUE, attempt light type coercion (default = TRUE).

title: Pull a NYS Open Data dataset from the NYS Open Data catalog — nys_pull_dataset
text: tibble.

title: Pull a NYS Open Data dataset from the NYS Open Data catalog — nys_pull_dataset
text: Dataset keys generated dataset titles using `janitor::make_clean_names()`. keys derived live catalog metadata, dataset UIDs stable option.

title: Pull a NYS Open Data dataset from the NYS Open Data catalog — nys_pull_dataset
code: if (interactive() && curl::has_internet()) {   # Pull by key   nys_pull_dataset("311_service_requests", limit = 3)    # Pull by UID   nys_pull_dataset("28gk-bu58", limit = 3)    # Filters   nys_pull_dataset("28gk-bu58", limit = 3, filters = list(award_name = "MBA"))  }
